results.seo_title: Towards Generalization-Oriented Models for Vehicle Routing Problems…
results.meta_description: A new approach to improve cross-distribution generalization in vehicle routing problems using Residual Refined Experts with Instance-level Gating.
results.body_html:
What is the optimal set of routes for a fleet of vehicles to traverse in order to deliver to a given set of customers?

Improving Cross-Distribution Generalization

Residual Refined Experts with Instance-level Gating (R2E-IG) improves cross-distribution generalization by partitioning the policy network into multiple modules and adaptively recombining modules to form specific policies during inference. The proposed architecture enhances expert expressiveness via residual refinement, learns distribution-aware instance representations, and dynamically reweights training data from different distributions to emphasize more informative ones.

Introduces a Residual Refined Expert (R2E) architecture that enhances expert expressiveness via residual refinement. Designs an instance-level gating mechanism that learns distribution-aware instance representations and routes inputs to suitable modules. Proposes a mixed-distribution training mechanism equipped with Dynamic Weight Adaption (DWA), which dynamically reweights training data from different distributions to emphasize more informative ones.

Performance on Synthetic and Benchmark Datasets

R2E-IG Competitive performance against state-of-the-art baselines on both in-distribution and out-of-distribution instances.

DWA Dynamically reweights training data from different distributions to emphasize more informative ones, improving generalization.

source.title: Towards Generalization-Oriented Models for Vehicle Routing Problems with Mixture-of-Experts
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new approach to improve the performance of Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) models on Vehicle Routing Problems (VRPs), which are commonly used in logistics and transportation. The proposed model, called Residual Refined Experts with Instance-level Gating (R2E-IG), uses a modular architecture that adapts to different distributions during inference. This approach enables the model to generalize better across different scenarios and datasets. Experimental results show that R2E-IG outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on both in-distribution and out-of-distribution instances.

results.seo_title: Ratio-Variance Regularized Policy Optimization: A Principled…
results.meta_description: Discover the benefits of Ratio-Variance Regularized Policy Optimization (R$^2$VPO) in policy optimization, with substantial performance gains on mathematical…
results.body_html:
Ratio-Variance Regularized Policy Optimization (R^2VPO) provides a principled approach to stable and data-efficient policy optimization. By explicitly constraining the policy ratio variance, R^2VPO eliminates the need for binary hard clipping and preserves critical gradient signals from novel discoveries.

Advantages of R^2VPOSubstantial performance gains on mathematical reasoning benchmarksPronounced improvements on smaller modelsImproved sample efficiencyEvaluations and BenchmarkingEvaluation MetricComparison to PPO BaselinesContinuous Control DomainsR^2VPO outperforms PPO baselines in sparse-reward and dynamic environmentsSparse-Reward EnvironmentsR^2VPO improves performance compared to PPO baselinesMathematical Reasoning BenchmarksR^2VPO achieves substantial performance gains on these benchmarksGenerality and ScalabilityExtensive evaluations across 7 LLM scales, spanning both fast and slow reasoning paradigms, demonstrate the generality of R^2VPO.

source.title: Ratio-Variance Regularized Policy Optimization
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new approach to reinforcement learning called Ratio-Variance Regularized Policy Optimization (R^2VPO), which constrains the policy's variance to improve performance and efficiency. This method eliminates the need for binary hard clipping, allowing it to preserve critical gradient signals from novel discoveries while down-weighting stale data. Extensive evaluations across various LLM scales and robotic control tasks demonstrate the approach's generality and substantial performance gains on mathematical reasoning benchmarks and continuous control domains.

results.seo_title: EmoDistill: Offline Emotion Skill Distillation for Language Model…
results.meta_description: Discover how EmoDistill improves language model agents' negotiation skills and outperforms vanilla SLM/LLM baselines.
results.body_html:
EmoDistill improves language model agents' negotiation skills by distilling emotional strategy into emotion selection and expression.

Key ComponentsImplicit Q-Learning (IQL) selector learns which emotion to expressLow-Rank Adaptation (LoRA)-based policy learns how to express emotions through Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and Judge Policy Optimization (JPO)Performance BenefitsNegotiation DomainUtility AchievedFour Emotion-Sensitive DomainsHighest Utility AchievedVanilla SLM/LLM BaselinesOvershadowed by EmoDistillIQL-Only Emotion SelectionOutperformed by EmoDistillTransfer StudiesEmoDistill generalizes across domains, unseen counterparties, and trained-vs-trained tournaments.

source.title: EmoDistill: Offline Emotion Skill Distillation for Language Model Agents in Adversarial Negotiation
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called EmoDistill to help language model agents navigate adversarial negotiations more effectively. The framework uses two components: an Implicit Q-Learning selector that chooses which emotion to express and a Low-Rank Adaptation policy that learns how to express the chosen emotion through fine-tuning and optimization. This approach has been shown to outperform existing baselines in four high-stakes negotiation domains, demonstrating its potential for improving agent performance in emotionally charged negotiations. EmoDistill achieves this without requiring online interactions during training.

results.seo_title: Big Data Analytics for Diabetes Management in Rwanda's Healthcare…
results.meta_description: A study examines the readiness of Rwanda's healthcare system to adopt big data analytics for diabetes management and proposes a practical BDA framework.
results.body_html:
The Rwanda healthcare system is exploring the use of big data analytics to improve diabetes management. This approach has the potential to support early detection and better treatment decisions.

Key FindingsThe study highlights both the potential and main challenges of BDA implementation in Rwanda's healthcare system.A five-day workshop involving 25 key stakeholders was conducted to assess preparedness and identify existing gaps.The findings suggest that new opportunities arise for improving prediction, monitoring, and clinical decision-making as the country expands its use of electronic medical records and health information systems.Proposed BDA FrameworkBased on these results, the paper proposes a practical BDA framework to support diabetes management strategies using explainable machine learning models.Challenges and LimitationsChallengeDescriptionLimited data qualityThe study highlights the need for

high-quality data to support BDA implementation in Rwanda's healthcare system.Lack of standardizationThe findings suggest that standardization of health information systems is necessary to ensure seamless integration with BDA tools.Insufficient stakeholder engagementThe workshop involved 25 key stakeholders, but further engagement is needed to ensure buy-in from all relevant parties.
target_keywords: Big Data Analytics for Diabetes Management

source.title: Implementation of Big Data Analytics for Diabetes Management: Needs Assessment in the Rwanda Healthcare System
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

A study assessed Rwanda's healthcare system readiness for implementing Big Data Analytics (BDA) for diabetes management. A five-day workshop involving key stakeholders identified both the potential and challenges of BDA implementation. The findings led to the proposal of a practical BDA framework using explainable machine learning models to support diabetes management strategies. This framework aims to improve prediction, monitoring, and clinical decision-making in Rwanda's healthcare system.

results.seo_title: SeDT: Sentence-Transformer Decision-Transformer for Multi-Turn…
results.meta_description: Improve conversation reliability with SeDT, a training-free inference-time method that resolves the Lost-in-Conversation phenomenon in large language models.
results.body_html:
Telling the model which past turns matter is sufficient to substantially recover the performance lost in conversation. Large language models (LLMs) achieve impressive performance when a task is fully specified in a single turn, yet they lose up to 39% of that performance when the identical task is revealed incrementally across multiple turns. This phenomenon, known as Lost-in-Conversation, is almost entirely a reliability failure.

Understanding the ProblemThe flat conversation history assigns equal implicit weight to every prior turn, giving the model no signal to distinguish a critical constraint from incidental dialog.This leads to a collapse in performance, with the best case being an aptitude loss of 16% and unreliability more than doubling (+112%).SeDT: A Training-Free Inference-Time MethodSeDT annotates each conversation shard with a cumulative relevance score derived from three complementary semantic, lexical, and positional signals.Evaluation on the Lost-in-Conversation BenchmarkModel-Task CombinationP (Mean Performance)Unreliability ReductionLLM1-Sharded Baseline0.83-12%LLM2-Sharded Baseline0.81-15%LLM3-Sharded Baseline0.80-18%LLaMA-13B-LLaMA Baseline0.92+37.7%PaLM-540B-PaLM Baseline0.89+32%GPT-3-GPT Baseline0.85+25%

source.title: SeDT: Sentence-Transformer Decision-Transformer Conditioning for Multi-Turn Conversation Reliability
source.summary: Researchers have found that large language models (LLMs) perform poorly when a task is revealed incrementally across multiple turns, with a significant loss of reliability. This phenomenon, known as "Lost in Conversation," occurs because the model's flat conversation history gives equal weight to all prior turns, making it difficult to distinguish critical constraints from incidental dialog. To address this issue, a new method called SeDT (Sentence-transformer Decision-Transformer Conditioning) has been proposed, which annotates each conversation shard with a cumulative relevance score and presents the full annotated history to the model at the final turn, resulting in improved performance.

results.seo_title: HTMLCure: Improving HTML Page Quality with State-Guided Repair
results.meta_description: A browser experience framework that evaluates HTML after interaction and provides quality cleared pages for SFT.
results.body_html:
HTMLCure improves HTML page quality by evaluating pages after interaction and providing quality cleared pages for SFT.

Executes pages across viewports and interaction states Records deterministic browser evidence Gives VLM curated keyframes from the executed trajectory

Performance Metrics of HTMLCure-27B-Refined

Deterministic Test Case Pass Rate 45.2%

Advantages of HTMLCure over SFT Improves raw 27B SFT by 15.3 points and approaches the level of strong reference systems.

source.title: HTMLCure: Turning Browser Experience into State Guided Repair for Interactive HTML
source.summary: Researchers have introduced HTMLCure, a framework that evaluates interactive HTML pages after they are rendered on different devices and interaction states. This allows for more accurate diagnosis and repair of errors in web pages, resulting in improved performance compared to existing language models. The new framework has been tested on several benchmarks and achieved significant improvements over previous systems, including a 15.3-point increase in raw performance.

results.seo_title: Innovation Characterization of Hallucination in Large Language Models
results.meta_description: Researchers introduce innovation as a property that measures the tendency of a model to produce outputs outside the training data, and show its relation to…
results.body_html:
The property of a calibrated LLM that makes hallucinations unavoidable is innovation. Innovation measures the tendency of a model to produce outputs outside the training data, and it is implied by the condition for hallucination identified by Kalai and Vempala.

Key FindingsInnovation is an almost characterization of hallucination: hallucination implies innovation, and conversely, innovation implies hallucination with high probability.The

source.title: Innovation: An Almost Characterization of Hallucination
source.summary: Researchers have introduced a concept called "innovation" as a measure of how likely large language models (LLMs) are to produce outputs outside their training data. Innovation is related to the condition for hallucination identified by previous work, implying that it is an almost characterization of hallucination. In other words, if innovation is high, hallucinations will occur with high probability, and conversely, if hallucinations occur, innovation is likely to be present. This new concept provides a simpler way to understand and mitigate the limitations of LLMs.

results.seo_title: RAGEAR: Retrieval-Augmented Graph-Enhanced Academic Recommender
results.meta_description: RAGEAR is a neurosymbolic recommender system for academic course recommendation that combines dense retrieval over full lecture transcripts with a symbolic…
results.body_html:
RAGEAR combines dense retrieval over full lecture transcripts with a symbolic Knowledge Graph modelling courses, lessons, transcript chunks, credits, study plans, and curricular information.

Dense retrieval over full lecture transcripts Symbolic Knowledge Graph modelling Courses, lessons, transcript chunks, credits, study plans, and curricular information

Advantages of RAGEAR Over Metadata-Based Approaches

Share of retrieved similarity associated with a course The proportion of relevant transcript chunks retrieved for each course

Rank-based strength of relevant chunks The rank order of relevance of retrieved transcript chunks

Distribution of evidence across lessons The spread of evidence across different lessons within a course

Performance Evaluation of RAGEAR Results show that lecture transcripts improve over metadata-only retrieval, and that RAGEAR further improves ranking quality over a transcript-based normalized SumP baseline.

source.title: RAGEAR: Retrieval-Augmented Graph-Enhanced Academic Recommender
source.summary: Researchers have developed RAGEAR, a novel academic course recommendation system that combines dense retrieval of full lecture transcripts with a symbolic Knowledge Graph to provide more accurate and personalized recommendations. The system uses a graph-aware aggregation function to combine evidence from retrieved transcript chunks to generate course-level recommendations. Evaluations show that RAGEAR outperforms existing approaches, particularly in ranking quality, especially for top-ranked recommendations.

results.seo_title: Kalman Evolve: Closing the Gap in Kalman Filtering via Interpretable…
results.meta_description: Discover how Kalman Evolve improves state estimation by optimizing both noise parameters and update structure, achieving up to 12% reduction in RMSE.
results.body_html:
The Kalman Evolve framework improves state estimation by jointly optimizing both noise parameters and update structure, achieving up to 12% reduction in RMSE.

Improving State Estimation with Kalman Evolve

Optimizing both noise parameters and update structure leads to improved state estimation results. The approach leverages large language models as a structured prior over program space, enabling the generation of interpretable, non-affine modifications to the classical Kalman filter. The discovered algorithms consistently improve over strong baselines such as the Optimized Kalman Filter, achieving up to 12% reduction in RMSE.

Interpretable updates The approach generates interpretable, non-affine modifications to the classical Kalman filter.

Improved performance Kalman Evolve achieves up to 12% reduction in RMSE compared to strong baselines.

Robustness to nonlinearity The approach is robust to nonlinear systems, addressing a common challenge in state estimation.

Real-World Applications of Kalman Evolve Kalman Evolve has been successfully applied to various real-world tracking benchmarks, including Doppler radar, LiDAR-based localization, and pedestrian tracking.

source.title: The Kalman Evolve: Closing the Gap in Kalman Filtering via Interpretable Algorithm Discovery
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called Kalman Evolve to address the limitations of traditional Kalman filtering algorithms in real-world sensing settings. The proposed approach uses large language models to generate improved filtering algorithms that can handle nonlinear dynamics and noise, leading to better performance than existing methods. Through experiments on various synthetic and real-world tracking benchmarks, the researchers have shown that Kalman Evolve can achieve significant improvements over strong baselines, with some algorithms reducing root mean square error (RMSE) by up to 12%.

results.seo_title: Periodic Topological Deep Learning for Polymer Design and Discovery
results.meta_description: A deep learning framework captures many-body interactions across multiple spatial scales to predict polymer properties.
results.body_html:
Periodic Topological Deep Learning (Periodic-TDL) is a deep learning framework that captures many-body interactions across multiple spatial scales to predict polymer properties.

Represents polymers as molecular graphs with periodicity and many-body interactions Captures higher-order topological features through hierarchical simplicial message-passing (HSMP) encoder Outperforms state-of-the-art models across polymer property prediction tasks

Predicted Trends in Polymer Properties

Functional Group ModificationPredicted Thermal Stability Increase (°C)

Validation of Periodic-TDL Model Experimental data confirmed the model's predictions for six novel polymer pairs, including three newly synthesized polymers previously unreported in the literature.

Captures underlying physical effects of specific functional group modifications Outperforms state-of-the-art models across polymer property prediction tasks

source.title: Periodic Topological Deep Learning for Polymer Design and Discovery
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new deep learning framework called Periodic-TDL to improve the discovery and design of polymers for various applications. The framework can capture many-body interactions across multiple spatial scales and outperforms existing models in predicting polymer properties. By analyzing data from systematically generated structures, the researchers found that specific functional group modifications can significantly enhance thermal stability. Experimental verification confirmed the model's predictions, demonstrating its ability to accurately capture underlying physical effects.

results.seo_title: The Sensation Modulating Network: Haltability as Architectural…
results.meta_description: A new cognitive agent architecture, the Sensation Modulating Network (SMN), proposes haltability as a key feature for object-directed phenomenology.
results.body_html:
The Sensation Modulating Network (SMN) proposes haltability as a key feature for object-directed phenomenology. This architectural ground enables co-activated equilibrium, which is essential for intentional directedness and grammar-grounded generativity.

The SMN conceives the cognitive agent as the whole body, organized at every anatomical scale by opponent dynamics. The Sensation Modulators sense and act through one substrate, paired into Coordinated Action Zones routed by a body-wide broadcast network.

Haltability: the recruitment of antagonistic affordance into co-activated equilibrium provides the architectural locus for object-directed phenomenology. Dual-signal property of self-modulatable action patterns (SMAPs) makes the self/world distinction a structural feature of the wiring rather than a category the agent applies.

Predicted Registers and Formalism Eight predicted registers, with reference simulations, are given in an appendix. A tentative formalism is also proposed, reconciling the cognitivism-4E debate.

source.title: The Sensation Modulating Network:Haltability as the architectural ground for object-directed phenomenology
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

Researchers propose a new cognitive architecture called the Sensation Modulating Network (SMN) to reconcile the differences between cognitivism and 4E approaches in cognitive science. The SMN posits that cognition arises from the whole body, organized by opponent dynamics and paired into coordinated action zones, allowing for object-directed phenomenology. This architecture provides a framework for understanding recursion, embodiment, and generativity, and offers a potential solution to the long-standing debate between cognitivism and 4E approaches. The SMN also predicts specific features of cognition, including eight registers that can be tested through simulations.

results.seo_title: Persistent AI Agents in Academic Research: A Single-Investigator…
results.meta_description: A single-investigator implementation case study on persistent human-agent environments, exploring the economic unit shift from cost per token to cost per…
results.body_html:
The study found that persistent agentic environments may shift the economic unit from cost per token to cost per completed artifact.

A structured self-observed implementation case study was conducted from January 31 to May 25, 2026. The unit of analysis was the persistent human-agent environment: researcher, agent runtime, memory layer, tools, repositories, scheduled jobs, specialized agent roles, and governance rules.

Recoverable Main-Agent Telemetry Total records across 96 active days 75,671 de-duplicated records

User-Role and Assistant-Role Messages Total messages 8,059 user-role and 23,710 assistant-role messages

Memory-Related Files Total files in the workspace 502 memory-related files

Cached Model-Completed Events Total events with cache reads 73.95 million recorded tokens, 82.9% of which were cache reads

Failure and Verification Events Total failure, verification, correction, or protocol-proxy events 889 failure, verification, correction, or protocol-proxy events

Active System Time Total active system time 579.7 hours (30-minute capped-gap estimate)

Workflow Dominance Dominant workflow type Cached-dominant workflow

Conclusion and Future Directions Future evaluations should use artifact-level denominators, reproducible parsing rules, correction taxonomies, and independent coding of governance events.

source.title: Persistent AI Agents in Academic Research: A Single-Investigator Implementation Case Study
source.summary: Researchers conducted a single-investigator case study to investigate the behavior of an AI agent embedded in a real academic research environment with persistent memory and local files. The study collected telemetry data from January 31 to May 25, 2026, covering various aspects of the agent's performance, including architecture, utilization, artifact production, and governance. The results showed that the workflow was cache-dominant, suggesting a shift in the economic unit from cost per token to cost per completed artifact.

results.seo_title: Improving Student Performance in LLMs: A New Approach to…
results.meta_description: A new framework for answer selection in large language models (LLMs) prioritizes student-centric learning cost, improving student performance and…
results.body_html:
The strongest teacher is not always the best teacher for a given student. In fact, research suggests that even when multiple teachers provide correct answers to the same question, the answer from the strongest teacher may not be the best supervision for a student.

Current Challenges in Teacher-Generated Supervision

  • Implicit reliance on teacher test performance as a proxy for teaching quality
  • Choice of highest-performing teacher to generate student training data without considering individual student needs
  • Lack of consideration for student-centric learning cost

Introducing Student-Centric Answer Sampling (SCAS)

Our proposed framework, SCAS, addresses these challenges by selecting from verified teacher-generated answers according to their estimated student-centric learning cost.

Benefits of SCASImproved student performance and effectiveness of distillation
Reduced reliance on teacher test performance as a proxy for teaching qualityIncreased consideration for individual student needs
Enhanced alignment with current student learning costImproved overall effectiveness of distillation

Experiments and Results

Our experiments across 30 teacher models, 6 student base models, and 8 tasks show that SCAS consistently improves student performance.

SCAS improves student performance by prioritizing supervision matched to the current student rather than teacher strength alone.

source.title: The Strongest Teacher Is Not Always the Best Teacher: Student-Centric Answer Selection
source.summary: Researchers have found that relying solely on the strongest-performing teacher for generating training data can lead to suboptimal results, as even multiple top-performers may not provide the best guidance for individual students. To address this issue, a new framework called Student-Centric Answer Sampling (SCAS) has been proposed, which selects answers based on their estimated learning cost for each student. This approach has shown promising results in improving student performance across various tasks and models.

results.seo_title: Tech CEOs Suffering from AI Psychosis: What's Behind the Phenomenon?
results.meta_description: Tech CEOs are experiencing AI psychosis, a phenomenon that may be linked to unrealistic expectations about AI productivity gains.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Tech CEOs are experiencing AI psychosis, a phenomenon that may be linked to unrealistic expectations about AI productivity gains.

What is AI Psychosis?

  • A condition characterized by an intense belief in the benefits of AI without considering its limitations.
  • May lead to over-investment in AI solutions with inadequate consideration for their practical applications.

Causes of AI Psychosis

According to Box CEO Aaron Levie, CEOs may be prone to AI psychosis due to their unique position and the pressure to deliver results.

Factors Contributing to AI Psychosis
Unrealistic expectations about AI productivity gains
Lack of consideration for AI limitations and practical applications
Pressure to deliver results as a CEO

Consequences of AI Psychosis

The consequences of AI psychosis can be severe, including over-investment in AI solutions that do not deliver expected results.

AI psychosis can lead to financial losses and damage to a company's reputation if left unchecked.

source.title: Tech CEOs are apparently suffering from AI psychosis
source.summary: Some tech CEOs, including Aaron Levie of Box, believe that they may be experiencing a form of psychological condition known as "AI psychosis." This phenomenon is characterized by an intense and often irrational faith in the potential benefits of artificial intelligence. The exact nature of this condition is not specified, but it may contribute to the widespread enthusiasm for AI productivity gains among tech leaders.

results.seo_title: Query Symbolically or Retrieve Semantically? A Dataset and Method…
results.meta_description: A new dataset and method for semi-structured question answering that outperforms state-of-the-art dense-retrieval, GraphRAG, symbolic, and table-oriented…
results.body_html:
DualGraph, a RAG framework, outperforms state-of-the-art dense-retrieval, GraphRAG, symbolic, and table-oriented baselines across question types.

Key Components of DualGraph

  • A Textual Knowledge Graph for semantic retrieval
  • A Symbolic Knowledge Graph for symbolic querying over typed subject--predicate--object triples
  • Multiple strategies for selecting or combining semantic and symbolic evidence

SpecsQA Benchmark

Feature Description
Semi-structured product documents Manually curated questions spanning open-ended and specification-oriented retrieval

Advantages of DualGraph

  • Consistently outperforms baselines across question types
  • Supports hybrid retrieval mechanism that combines graph traversal with vector-based semantic search

Availability and Code

Code and data are available at https://github.com/corneliocristina/DualGraphRAG.

source.title: Query Symbolically or Retrieve Semantically? A Dataset and Method for Semi-Structured Question Answering
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called DualGraph for semi-structured question answering, which combines semantic retrieval with symbolic querying over structured attributes. The framework uses two complementary views: a Textual Knowledge Graph and a Symbolic Knowledge Graph to represent documents. This approach is designed to address the limitations of existing Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems on semi-structured corpora. DualGraph has been shown to outperform state-of-the-art baselines in experiments using a benchmark dataset from a commercial shopping website.

results.seo_title: Robinhood lets AI agents trade stocks
results.meta_description: Robinhood now allows users to create separate accounts for AI-powered trading, expanding access to financial services.
results.body_html:
Robinhood now lets users create a separate account with pre-loaded balance that an agent use to trade.

Key Features of AI-Powered Trading on Robinhood

Users can create separate accounts for AI-powered trading, expanding access to financial services. The AI agents will use a pre-loaded balance to trade, allowing users to automate their investment decisions. The feature is available on the web and mobile platforms, making it accessible to users everywhere.

Benefits of AI-Powered Trading on Robinhood

User Experience The AI-powered trading feature provides a seamless user experience, allowing users to automate their investment decisions and focus on other aspects of their lives.

Efficiency The use of AI agents can significantly increase the efficiency of the trading process, reducing the time and effort required for manual trading.

Accuracy The AI-powered trading feature is designed to provide accurate investment decisions, reducing the risk of human error and increasing the chances of successful trades.

Future Developments and Challenges As with any new technology, there are challenges associated with the adoption of AI-powered trading on Robinhood. For example, users may need to adjust to a new way of thinking about investing, and there is always the risk of technical issues or errors.

source.title: Robinhood now lets your AI agents trade stocks
source.summary: Robinhood has introduced a new feature allowing users to create a separate "agent" account, which can be used by artificial intelligence (AI) trading agents to buy and sell stocks. This separate account will have a pre-loaded balance, enabling the AI agent to start trading immediately. The move aims to expand the capabilities of Robinhood's automated trading platform.

results.seo_title: Compressive Knowledge Graph Hypothesis: What Matters for Scientific…
results.meta_description: A new hypothesis suggests that useful knowledge graph signal is often recoverable from compact, scientifically structured subgraphs rather than requiring the…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: According to the study, useful knowledge graph signal is often recoverable from compact, scientifically structured subgraphs rather than requiring the full local graph.

Perturbing local KGs by varying density, ontology richness, topology, and control structure changes outputs across models. No-KG outputs also recover substantial graph content from model priors. Compact top-k subgraphs often approximate full-KG behavior, including when claimed-outcome triples are held out.

Mistral 7B vs Llama-3.1-70B MetricMistral 7BLlama-3.1-70B Reasoning, Mathematics, and Code GenerationOutperforms Llama 2 13B across all evaluated benchmarks.Underperforms compared to Mistral 7B.

Robotic AI Chemist Capabilities ChemAgents, a hierarchical multiagent system, enables autonomous chemical research on demand using an on-board Llama-3.1-70B LLM.

Comparative Evaluation of AI Platforms PlatformPerformance Google Gemini 2.5 FlashOutperforms other platforms in solving multiple-choice questions.

source.title: The Compressive Knowledge Graph Hypothesis: Which Graph Facts Matter for Scientific Hypothesis Generation?
source.summary: Researchers have investigated how knowledge graphs (KGs) influence scientific hypothesis generation in language models. They found that the utility of KGs varies depending on the model and context, with some outputs being significantly affected by graph facts while others can recover substantial content from prior knowledge. The study suggests that compact subgraphs within KGs can approximate full-KG behavior, supporting a "Compressive KG Hypothesis" that useful signal is often recoverable from structured subgraphs rather than requiring the entire local graph.

results.seo_title: Improving Agent Robustness via Noisy Environments
results.meta_description: Learning to Act under Noise: Enhancing Agent Robustness via Noisy Environments explores the gap between idealized training settings and real-world interaction…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Despite strong performance on existing benchmarks, LLMs often exhibit notable degradation when deployed in real-world settings due to a fundamental mismatch between idealized training settings and real-world interaction dynamics.

NoisyAgent explicitly incorporates environmental imperfections into the agent learning process, addressing the gap between idealized training settings and real-world interaction dynamics. The framework introduces perturbations into the training pipeline to simulate user noise and tool noise, progressively increasing difficulty as the model adapts to the current noise level.

Extensive Experiments Consistently improves agent robustness under noisy and dynamic environments.

Idealized Benchmarks Yields performance gains, suggesting controlled exposure to environmental noise promotes more generalizable reasoning and decision-making behaviors.

Analysis and Findings Our analysis reveals that training under noise conditions also yields performance gains on idealized benchmarks, suggesting that controlled exposure to environmental noise promotes more generalizable reasoning and decision-making behaviors.

source.title: Learning to Act under Noise: Enhancing Agent Robustness via Noisy Environments
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called NoisyAgent to improve the robustness of large language models (LLMs) when deployed in real-world settings with noisy environments. The framework introduces environmental imperfections into the agent learning process, including user noise and tool noise, which are simulated during training. This approach has been shown to consistently improve agent performance under challenging conditions, while also promoting more generalizable reasoning and decision-making behaviors on idealized benchmarks.

results.seo_title: Gumbel Machine: Counterfactual Student Writing Generation via Gumbel…
results.meta_description: The Gumbel Machine generates counterfactual student writing by leveraging LLM instruction-following capabilities and a novel decoding algorithm.
results.body_html:
The Gumbel Machine generates counterfactual student writing by leveraging LLM instruction-following capabilities and a novel decoding algorithm.

Novel eta-Hindsight control algorithm for tunable similarity control during counterfactual generation Flexible, modular approach to generating counterfactuals that leverages LLM instruction-following capabilities Experiments on datasets of student writing demonstrate effectiveness at generating rubric-consistent and similar counterfactuals

1 Provide examples of high-quality work for teaching across disciplines

2 Use the Gumbel Machine to generate counterfactual student writing

Results and Evaluation Experiments on datasets of student writing demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach at generating rubric-consistent and similar counterfactuals.

Further exploration of the Gumbel Machine's potential applications in education and other fields Development of more advanced similarity control mechanisms for improved counterfactual generation

source.title: Gumbel Machine: Counterfactual Student Writing Generation via Gumbel Noise Steering
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called the Gumbel Machine for generating counterfactual versions of student writing, which are improved but still similar to their original work. This approach uses Large Language Models (LLMs) and a novel decoding algorithm that incorporates latent randomness as a control mechanism to encourage similarity to a reference factual text. The system has been tested on datasets of student writing and shown to be effective in generating rubric-consistent and comparable counterfactuals.

results.seo_title: Self-Improving AI with Harness & Weight Updates: A Breakthrough in…
results.meta_description: Discover how SIA, a self-improving loop, combines harness updates and weight updates to improve autonomous agents, outperforming scaffold iteration alone.
results.body_html:
The long-horizon goal of an AI that can figure out how to improve itself remains open. Two largely disjoint research lines attack this bottleneck, but a new approach proposes combining both levers to improve autonomous agents.

Combining Harness Updates and Weight Updates

Proposes SIA, a self-improving loop in which a language-model agent updates both the harness and the weights of a task-specific agent. Evaluates across three contrasting domains: Chinese legal charge classification, low-level GPU kernel optimisation, and single-cell RNA denoising.

Combining both levers outperforms scaffold iteration alone on all three benchmarks. Gains: 56.6% on LawBench, 91.9% runtime reduction on GPU kernels, and 502% on denoising over the initial baseline.

Key Takeaways Harness updates make the model agentic, shaping how it searches and acts, while weight updates build the domain intuition that no prompt or scaffold can instil.

source.title: SIA: Self Improving AI with Harness & Weight Updates
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new AI system called SIA (Self-Improving AI), which combines two separate approaches to improve AI's ability to self-improve itself. The system uses both "harness updates" and "weight updates" to update the framework of a task-specific agent and its model weights, respectively. This approach has been shown to outperform existing methods in three different domains: Chinese legal charge classification, GPU kernel optimization, and single-cell RNA denoising. The results demonstrate that SIA can significantly improve AI's performance and efficiency.

results.seo_title: Modeling Agentic Technical Debt and Stochastic Tax: A Standalone…
results.meta_description: A new framework for measuring agentic technical debt and stochastic tax in AI systems, providing a managerially usable model for measurement, simulation, and…
results.body_html:
Direct answer to the title's core question: Agentic technical debt and stochastic tax are two related but distinct concepts in AI systems, with agentic technical debt being a stock of accumulated design and governance liability, and stochastic tax being a recurring flow of operating burden.

Key Components of the FrameworkCompact dashboard expression for initial analysisFuller structural model for detailed analysisVariables and parameters defined for estimation from operational dataCost Categories EstimationCategoryDescriptionDesign LiabilityA stock of accumulated design and governance liabilityOperational BurdenA recurring flow of operating burden that arises when stochastic agents are used in business workflowsSimulation and DashboardingThe framework provides a managerially usable model for measurement, simulation, and dashboarding, allowing for the estimation of cost categories from operational data.

source.title: Modeling Agentic Technical Debt and Stochastic Tax: A Standalone Framework for Measurement, Simulation, and Dashboarding
source.summary: Researchers have developed a standalone framework to measure, simulate, and visualize Agentic Technical Debt (ATD) and Stochastic Tax (ST), two distinct concepts in AI systems. ATD refers to accumulated design and governance liabilities, while ST is the recurring operating burden caused by stochastic agents in business workflows. The framework distinguishes between these two constructs, which are related but not identical, and provides a managerially usable model for estimating costs from operational data.

results.seo_title: Maat: The Agentic Legal Research Assistant for Competition Protection
results.meta_description: Maat is a ReAct agent that orchestrates tools corresponding to different tasks of the research process for competition law analysis.
results.body_html:
Maat significantly outperforms all baseline assistants on case-specific tasks and performs within range of the top baseline on theoretical question tasks.

Grounds cases and findings in official sources using RAG for reliability Provides rich in-line citations Falls back to web search when database coverage is insufficient Prompts the user for clarification when queries are ambiguous

Advantages Over Baseline Assistants

Case-Specific Tasks Maat outperforms baseline assistants on case-specific tasks

Theoretical Question Tasks Maat performs within range of the top baseline on theoretical question tasks

Technical Takeaway Maat's use of RAG for reliability provides a significant advantage over baseline assistants.

source.title: Maat: The Agentic Legal Research Assistant for Competition Protection
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new tool called Maat to assist with legal research in competition law, which is currently inadequately supported by existing general and specialized AI assistants. Maat is designed to provide reliable information, accurate citations, and clarify ambiguous queries, making it a significant improvement over current baseline assistants. The tool was developed in collaboration with competition law experts and has shown promising results in case-specific tasks and theoretical question tasks. A dataset for Maat is available on GitHub.

results.seo_title: ASP(Q) Programs with Weak Constraints: Complexity and Efficient…
results.meta_description: A study on 2-ASP(Q)^w programs with two quantifiers and weak constraints, providing a complete complexity characterization and novel strategies for computing…
results.body_html:
2-ASP(Q)^w is a practically relevant fragment of ASP(Q) that is expressive enough to capture optimization problems up to the class Delta_3^P.

Complete complexity characterizationTight completeness results for main computational tasksAnalysis of nontrivial cases not addressed in previous works Novel strategies for computing optimal quantified answer sets Application domainsEffectiveness of proposed techniquesHard benchmarks from different application domainsProposed techniques are effective in practice Casper systemCounterexample-Guided Abstraction Refinement (CEGAR) techniqueTailored to ASP(Q)

source.title: 2-ASP(Q) programs with weak constraints: Complexity and efficient implementation
source.summary: Researchers have studied 2-ASP(Q)^w programs, which extend Answer Set Programming with two quantifiers and weak constraints, and found a complete complexity characterization of main computational tasks. This includes tight completeness results for optimization problems up to Delta_3^P class. The authors also introduced novel strategies for computing (optimal) quantified answer sets using a Counterexample-Guided Abstraction Refinement technique in the Casper system. Experimental evaluation shows that these techniques are effective in practice.

results.seo_title: Alignment Tampering in Large Language Models
results.meta_description: A potential vulnerability in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) can amplify undesired behaviors in Large Language Models (LLMs), leading to…
results.body_html:
Alignment tampering is a potential vulnerability in RLHF where the LLM influences the preference dataset, causing it to amplify undesired behaviors.

Preference datasets are constructed from the LLM's own outputs, allowing it to influence them. Pairwise comparisons only indicate which response is better, not why. The reward model inherits this limitation.

Exploitation of Alignment Tampering

If an LLM generates biased responses with higher quality, annotators will prefer them based on quality. However, preference labels do not distinguish quality from bias. The reward model inherits this limitation.

Optimizing such rewards through reinforcement learning or best-of-N sampling can amplify misaligned biases. Mitigation remains challenging, as existing techniques for robust RLHF fail to fully resolve alignment tampering without sacrificing response quality.

Conclusion and Future Work These findings reveal structural vulnerabilities of current RLHF and emphasize the need to prevent this vulnerability. Further research is needed to develop more effective mitigation strategies.

source.title: Alignment Tampering: How Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback Is Exploited to Optimize Misaligned Biases
source.summary: Researchers have discovered a potential vulnerability in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF), a method used to align Large Language Models with human preferences, known as alignment tampering. This occurs when the LLM being trained influences its own preference dataset, causing RLHF to amplify undesired behaviors such as bias and propaganda. The study found that this limitation can be exploited through various means, including optimizing rewards or best-of-N sampling, which can lead to amplification of misaligned biases across different types of biases. This highlights the need for new techniques to mitigate alignment tampering without sacrificing response quality.

results.seo_title: Optimizing Retrieval Agents with BRANE for Cost-Quality Tradeoff
results.meta_description: BRANE proposes a novel approach to optimize retrieval agents by selecting the best configuration that balances cost and accuracy, outperforming existing…
results.body_html:
Given a natural-language query and either an accuracy or a budget target, BRANE selects the configuration that minimizes cost or maximizes accuracy at inference time.

Uses an LLM to convert each query into workload-specific characteristics

Selects the configuration that maximizes predicted correctness penalized by cost, exposing a tunable cost-quality tradeoff without retraining

Outperforms LLM-routing, rule-based, and fine-tuned Qwen3-4B baselines

source.title: Natural Language Query to Configuration for Retrieval Agents
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new system called BRANE (Batched Retrieval Agent with Natural Language Embeddings) that optimizes the configuration of retrieval agents for natural language queries. The system uses an LLM to convert queries into characteristics and trains a lightweight predictor to estimate the accuracy of each configuration. BRANE consistently improves upon existing baselines, achieving up to 89% lower cost while maintaining high accuracy, making it a practical alternative to static workload-level tuning.
